title: Tensions Rise in Kenya Over US Ebola Quarantine Plans
subtitle: Protests continue as health officials struggle to control a growing outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo.

summary.en: Kenya is experiencing violent protests against a planned US quarantine center for Ebola patients. The facility is intended to treat Americans exposed to the virus in the Democratic Republic of the Congo. Meanwhile, the Ebola outbreak in the Congo has reached 676 confirmed cases. Health experts warn that the virus could spread further if strong action is not taken soon. Families in Kenya are now mourning the loss of loved ones caught in the recent unrest.
summary.zh: 肯亞正因一座計畫中的美國伊波拉病患隔離中心而爆發暴力抗議。該設施旨在治療在剛果民主共和國接觸過病毒的美國人。與此同時，剛果的伊波拉疫情已累計六百七十六起確診病例。衛生專家警告，若不儘快採取強硬行動，病毒恐將進一步擴散。肯亞的家庭正為在近期動亂中失去摯愛而哀悼。

paragraphs.en: The situation in Kenya has become increasingly difficult as citizens protest against a new quarantine center. This facility is being built to house US citizens who may have been exposed to the Ebola virus while working in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC). According to Ars Technica, the protests in the town of Nanyuki have turned violent, leading to tragic results for local families. Reports indicate that a mother recently found the body of her 17-year-old son following clashes between police and demonstrators.
paragraphs.zh: 肯亞的局勢日益嚴峻，當地民眾正針對一座新建的隔離中心發起抗議。該設施旨在收容在剛果民主共和國（DRC）工作期間，可能接觸過伊波拉病毒的美國公民。根據科技媒體 Ars Technica 報導，位於南尼基鎮（Nanyuki）的抗議活動已演變成暴力衝突，並對當地家庭造成悲劇性的後果。據報導，在一場警方與示威者的衝突後，一名母親發現了她十七歲兒子的遺體。

paragraphs.en: These protests began after news spread that the facility would be used to treat Americans. Many local residents fear that the center will bring the deadly virus into their community. Despite a court order to stop construction, work on the site has continued, which has further angered the public. Previous reports from Ars Technica confirmed that at least one demonstrator has already died during these confrontations. The community remains deeply divided, with the Kenyan government supporting the project while many citizens continue to express their strong opposition.
paragraphs.zh: 這些抗議活動始於該設施將用於治療美國人的消息傳開之後。許多當地居民擔心，這座中心會將致命病毒帶入他們的社區。儘管法院已下令停止施工，但現場工程仍在持續，這進一步激怒了民眾。Ars Technica 先前的報導證實，在這些對峙中，至少已有一名示威者喪生。目前社區內部依然嚴重分裂，肯亞政府支持該計畫，但許多公民持續表達強烈反對。

paragraphs.en: While these protests continue in Kenya, the health crisis in the DRC is growing. Ars Technica reports that as of Thursday, June 11, there are 676 confirmed cases of Ebola and 136 deaths. Additionally, there are 119 suspected cases. The virus has also spread to Uganda, where 19 cases and two deaths have been recorded. This outbreak is caused by the Bundibugyo strain of the Ebola virus. It is already considered the third largest outbreak in history, but experts worry it could become much worse.
paragraphs.zh: 在肯亞抗議活動持續的同時，剛果民主共和國的健康危機正不斷擴大。Ars Technica 報導指出，截至六月十一日星期四，伊波拉病毒的確診病例已達六百七十六例，死亡人數為一百三十六人，此外還有一百一十九起疑似病例。病毒也已擴散至烏干達，當地記錄到十九起病例及兩起死亡案例。此次疫情是由伊波拉病毒的邦迪布焦（Bundibugyo）病毒株所引起。這已被視為史上第三大規模的疫情，但專家擔憂情況可能會變得更糟。

paragraphs.en: Investigators are working hard to understand how the outbreak started. They believe a funeral in the town of Mongbwalu on February 4 may have been a key event where the virus spread to many people. A 44-year-old pastor died there, and although he was never tested for Ebola, his symptoms were consistent with the disease. More than 80 people attended the funeral, and many of them became sick shortly after. Within two weeks, nearly 50 deaths were reported in that area, showing how quickly the virus can move through a community.
paragraphs.zh: 調查人員正致力於釐清疫情的起源。他們認為二月四日在蒙布瓦盧鎮（Mongbwalu）舉行的一場葬禮，可能是病毒大規模擴散的關鍵事件。一名四十四歲的牧師在該地過世，雖然他生前未接受伊波拉病毒檢測，但其症狀與該疾病相符。當時有超過八十人參加了葬禮，其中許多人在隨後不久便發病。兩週內，該地區通報了近五十起死亡案例，顯示病毒在社區中傳播的速度極快。

paragraphs.en: The US Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) has created a model to predict how the outbreak might develop. Their findings suggest that if health officials do not act quickly with contact tracing—the process of finding everyone who has been near an infected person—and case isolation, the situation could become as severe as the 2014–2016 West Africa outbreak. That event resulted in over 28,000 cases and 11,000 deaths. The CDC emphasizes that strong public health interventions are necessary to stop the virus from spreading further.
paragraphs.zh: 美國疾病管制與預防中心（CDC）建立了一套模型，以預測疫情可能的發展。研究結果顯示，若衛生官員未能迅速採取接觸者追蹤（即找出所有曾與感染者近距離接觸過的人）及病例隔離措施，情況可能會變得與二零一四至二零一六年間的西非疫情一樣嚴重。那次疫情導致超過兩萬八千人感染，並造成一萬一千人死亡。美國疾管中心強調，必須採取強而有力的公共衛生干預措施，才能阻止病毒進一步擴散。

paragraphs.en: For now, the tension in Kenya remains high. The combination of public fear regarding the quarantine center and the rising number of Ebola cases in the region has created a very unstable environment. As the international community watches the situation in the DRC, the local impact of these health policies continues to be felt by the people of Nanyuki. The future of the quarantine center remains uncertain, as does the ability of health organizations to contain the growing threat of the virus.
paragraphs.zh: 目前，肯亞的緊張局勢依然高漲。公眾對隔離中心的恐懼，加上該地區伊波拉病例數不斷攀升，營造出一個極不穩定的環境。隨著國際社會密切關注剛果民主共和國的局勢，這些衛生政策對南尼基鎮居民造成的在地影響持續發酵。隔離中心的未來仍充滿不確定性，衛生組織是否有能力遏止日益嚴重的病毒威脅，亦是未知數。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To provide a place for someone or something to live or stay.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作名詞（房子），這裡作動詞用。文中：This facility is being built to house US citizens who may have been exposed to the Ebola virus while working in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC).

keyTerms.definitionEn: To become known or reach more people or places.
keyTerms.noteZh: 這裡指新聞或消息的流傳，而非物體散開。文中：These protests began after news spread that the facility would be used to treat Americans.

keyTerms.definitionEn: A system or plan used to predict or explain how something will happen.
keyTerms.noteZh: 這裡指科學上的預測模型，而非時尚模特兒或玩具模型。文中：The US Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) has created a model to predict how the outbreak might develop.

sources.name: Ars Technica
sources.title: Ebola cases in DRC rise to 676 as Kenya protests erupt over US plans

previousArticles.title: Tragedy in Kenya: Mother Finds Son After Ebola Center Protests
previousArticles.summaryEn: A mother in Nanyuki, Kenya, has discovered the body of her 17-year-old son following violent protests against a planned US Ebola quarantine center. The teenager was caught in clashes between police and demonstrators who fear the facility poses public health risks. While the government defends the project as a humanitarian necessity, local groups argue that the government has lacked transparency. Experts note that successful disease response requires working closely with local communities rather than ignoring their concerns. The incident marks the third death linked to the ongoing unrest surrounding the construction site.

previousArticles.title: Deadly Protests Continue in Kenya Over US Ebola Center
previousArticles.summaryEn: A demonstrator has died during a protest in Nanyuki, Kenya, against a planned US Ebola quarantine center. The facility is intended to treat Americans exposed to the virus in the Democratic Republic of Congo. Despite a court order to halt construction, work at the site has reportedly continued. President William Ruto has defended the project, citing the need to support a long-term partner. Local residents remain concerned about the risk of the virus entering their community.

previousArticles.title: Protests Turn Deadly in Kenya Over US Ebola Quarantine Center
previousArticles.summaryEn: Violent protests have erupted in the Kenyan town of Nanyuki over a new quarantine facility for US citizens. The center is designed to hold Americans exposed to the Ebola virus in the Democratic Republic of Congo. While the Kenyan government supports the project as a partnership with a long-term ally, local residents fear the virus could enter their country. A high court has attempted to stop construction, but work continues despite the legal challenges. Experts and activists remain divided on whether the facility truly improves global health security.

quiz.question: What is the primary reason for the violent protests in Nanyuki, Kenya?

options.A: The government's decision to close the border with the DRC.
options.B: The construction of a quarantine facility for US citizens.
options.C: The lack of medical supplies for local Kenyan citizens.
options.D: The death of a 44-year-old pastor at a local funeral.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案是 B。根據文章第一段與第二段，肯亞 Nanyuki 的抗議活動是因為當地正在興建一處隔離中心，專門用來安置可能接觸過伊波拉病毒的美國公民，這引發了當地居民對病毒傳播的恐懼。選項 A 並未提及；選項 C 雖然提到醫療資源，但非抗議主因；選項 D 提到的牧師葬禮是疫情擴散的原因，而非肯亞抗議的主因。

quiz.question: Based on the information provided, what can be inferred about the relationship between the funeral in Mongbwalu and the current outbreak?

options.A: The funeral was intentionally held to spread the virus among the community.
options.B: The funeral served as a 'superspreader' event that accelerated the virus's transmission.
options.C: The funeral was the only place where the Bundibugyo strain was identified.
options.D: The funeral attendees were the only people who received medical treatment.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案是 B。文章第四段指出，牧師葬禮後有 80 多人參加，隨後多人發病，兩週內出現近 50 例死亡，這顯示該葬禮是病毒大規模傳播的關鍵點，符合「超級傳播事件」的特徵。選項 A 錯誤，因為沒有證據顯示是蓄意；選項 C 錯誤，因為病毒也傳播到了烏干達；選項 D 錯誤，文中並未提及參加者都有得到治療。

quiz.question: In the final paragraph, what does the word 'unstable' most likely mean in the context of the situation in Kenya?

options.A: Physically weak or likely to collapse.
options.B: Unpredictable and prone to sudden, potentially dangerous changes.
options.C: Moving frequently from one location to another.
options.D: Lacking the necessary resources to function properly.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案是 B。在文中，unstable 用來形容肯亞目前的社會環境，結合了民眾對隔離中心的恐懼與疫情升溫的壓力，意味著局勢混亂且隨時可能惡化，具有高度不可預測性。選項 A 是指結構上的不穩；選項 C 指的是移動性；選項 D 雖然文中提到資源問題，但 unstable 在此處強調的是社會氛圍的動盪與不安，而非單指資源匱乏。

quiz.question: What is the central message of this article?

options.A: The US government is successfully containing the Ebola virus in the DRC.
options.B: The Kenyan government has effectively managed public fear regarding the virus.
options.C: The Ebola outbreak is a severe regional crisis that is causing both health and social instability.
options.D: Local protests in Nanyuki are the primary cause of the current Ebola outbreak in Uganda.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案是 C。全文涵蓋了剛果民主共和國的疫情擴散、CDC 的預測警告，以及肯亞因隔離中心引發的社會動盪，旨在說明這場伊波拉疫情不僅是公共衛生危機，也造成了嚴重的社會不安。選項 A 與事實相反，疫情正在擴大；選項 B 錯誤，因為肯亞民眾依然強烈反對；選項 D 因果倒置，抗議是疫情帶來的副作用，而非疫情的原因。
